revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=June 8, 2012}} [[File:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-michi, near [[Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].]] [[File:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-michi]]
'''Yamanobe-no-michi''' (山辺の道, {{small|ancient reading}}: Yamanobe-no-michi, {{small|archaic spelling}}: '''Yamanobe-michi''', {{small|old kanji spelling}}: '''山邊道''') is one of [[Japan]]'s representative [[old roads]]. It is part of the [[Ancient Roads of Yamato]] and is considered the '''oldest road in recorded [[Japanese history]]'''{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}, as well as the '''oldest existing road in Japan.'''
The road runs along the eastern edge of the [[Nara Basin]], weaving through the foothills at the base of the [[Kasuga Mountain]] escarpment from the foot of [[Mount Miwa]] in the southeast to the foothills of the northeast.
Although local governments often use the variant spelling "山の辺の道" ("Yama-no-be-no-michi"), this is not historically accurate based on [[toponymy]] or [[history]]. In contrast, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] uses the classical spelling "山辺道/山邊道."<ref group="*">Example: The official name for the Imperial Household Agency's management of the [[Andon-yama Kofun]] is "山辺道勾岡上陵" (Yamanobe-no-michi-no-Magari-no-Oka-no-ue-no-Misasagi).</ref>
== Overview ==
The '''''[[Nihon Shoki]]''''' mentions Yamanobe-no-michi in the context of Emperor [[Suijin]]'s burial: {{Quote box|blue|《Text from Nihon Shoki》葬于山邊道上陵 《Reading》{{Ruby|山辺道|Yamanobe-no-michi}}の{{Ruby|上|Hotori}}の{{Ruby|陵|Misasagi}}に{{Ruby|葬|So}}す。<ref name="Tsukuda-2005-pdf">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://tsukudaosamu.com/pdf/shin_kodaishi_04.pdf |title=崇神天皇と夫餘 - 日本古代史の復元 |author=Osamu Tsukuda|date=2005年冬|format=PDF |work=Restoration of Ancient Japanese History |accessdate=2019-04-01 }}</ref>{{rp|page=117}}}}
This suggests the road existed prior to the construction of Emperor Suijin's burial mound.
The '''''[[Kojiki]]''''' also mentions Yamanobe-no-michi in reference to both Emperor Suijin and Emperor [[Keikō]]: {{Quote box|red|《Text from Kojiki》御陵在山邊道勾之岡上也 《Reading》{{Ruby|御陵|Misasagi}}は{{Ruby|山辺道|Yamanobe-no-michi}}の{{Ruby|勾の岡|Magari-no-Oka}}の{{Ruby|上|Hotori}}に{{Ruby|在|Aru}}{{Ruby|也|Nari}}。<ref name="Tsukuda-2005-pdf" />}}
These references imply that Yamanobe-no-michi was already in use during the [[Kofun period]] (early 4th century). Some speculate that it existed in the late [[Yayoi period]] as a route connecting the [[Furu Ruins]] and the [[Makimuku Ruins]].
[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|Stone monument with a poem by [[Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] along the path.]] [[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|Poem monument of Kagehime near [[Wanishita Shrine]] in Tenri City.]]
The road connected [[So]] in the [[Yamato Province]] (later part of modern [[Nara]]) to Ishigami and Furu (modern [[Tenri]]), reaching Miwa (modern [[Sakurai]]). Stretching approximately 35 km, its width is less than 2 meters. Along its route are historical landmarks such as [[Isonokami Shrine]], [[Ōmiwa Shrine]], and ancient burial mounds like [[Suijin's Mausoleum]] and [[Keikō's Mausoleum]].
The area's geography, characterized by wetlands in the Nara Basin, influenced the road's layout, hugging the foothills to avoid swamps{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.
== Landmarks and Sites Along the Route == * [[Isonokami Shrine]] * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] * [[Hase Temple]] * [[Suijin Mausoleum]] * [[Keikō Mausoleum]] * [[Kurozuka Kofun]] * [[Kashihara Shrine]] * [[Tenri University Sankokan Museum]]
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Asai Kenji|title=The Dictionary of Roads|date=2001|publisher=Japanese Business Press|isbn=4-534-03315-X}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Rom International (ed.)|title=Surprising Knowledge of Roads|date=2005|publisher=Kawade Shobo Shinsha|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}
[[Category:Historical roads in Japan]] [[Category:Walking trails in Japan]] [[Category:Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese transportation]]
[[ja:山辺の道]]

{{Unreferenced|date=June 8, 2012}} [[File:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-michi, near [[Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].]] [[File:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-michi]]
'''Yamanobe-no-michi''' (山辺の道, {{small|ancient reading}}: Yamanobe-no-michi, {{small|archaic spelling}}: '''Yamanobe-michi''', {{small|old kanji spelling}}: '''山邊道''') is one of [[Japan]]'s representative [[old roads]]. It is part of the [[Ancient Roads of Yamato]] and is considered the '''oldest road in recorded [[Japanese history]]'''{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}, as well as the '''oldest existing road in Japan.'''
The road runs along the eastern edge of the [[Nara Basin]], weaving through the foothills at the base of the [[Kasuga Mountain]] escarpment from the foot of [[Mount Miwa]] in the southeast to the foothills of the northeast.
Although local governments often use the variant spelling "山の辺の道" ("Yama-no-be-no-michi"), this is not historically accurate based on [[toponymy]] or [[history]]. In contrast, the [[Imperial Household Agency]] uses the classical spelling "山辺道/山邊道."<ref group="*">Example: The official name for the Imperial Household Agency's management of the [[Andon-yama Kofun]] is "山辺道勾岡上陵" (Yamanobe-no-michi-no-Magari-no-Oka-no-ue-no-Misasagi).</ref>
== Overview ==
The '''''[[Nihon Shoki]]''''' mentions Yamanobe-no-michi in the context of Emperor [[Suijin]]'s burial: {{Quote box|blue|《Text from Nihon Shoki》葬于山邊道上陵 《Reading》{{Ruby|山辺道|Yamanobe-no-michi}}の{{Ruby|上|Hotori}}の{{Ruby|陵|Misasagi}}に{{Ruby|葬|So}}す。<ref name="Tsukuda-2005-pdf">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://tsukudaosamu.com/pdf/shin_kodaishi_04.pdf |title=崇神天皇と夫餘 - 日本古代史の復元 |author=Osamu Tsukuda|date=2005年冬|format=PDF |work=Restoration of Ancient Japanese History |accessdate=2019-04-01 }}</ref>{{rp|page=117}}}}
This suggests the road existed prior to the construction of Emperor Suijin's burial mound.
The '''''[[Kojiki]]''''' also mentions Yamanobe-no-michi in reference to both Emperor Suijin and Emperor [[Keikō]]: {{Quote box|red|《Text from Kojiki》御陵在山邊道勾之岡上也 《Reading》{{Ruby|御陵|Misasagi}}は{{Ruby|山辺道|Yamanobe-no-michi}}の{{Ruby|勾の岡|Magari-no-Oka}}の{{Ruby|上|Hotori}}に{{Ruby|在|Aru}}{{Ruby|也|Nari}}。<ref name="Tsukuda-2005-pdf" />}}
These references imply that Yamanobe-no-michi was already in use during the [[Kofun period]] (early 4th century). Some speculate that it existed in the late [[Yayoi period]] as a route connecting the [[Furu Ruins]] and the [[Makimuku Ruins]].
[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|Stone monument with a poem by [[Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]] along the path.]] [[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|Poem monument of Kagehime near [[Wanishita Shrine]] in Tenri City.]]
The road connected [[So]] in the [[Yamato Province]] (later part of modern [[Nara]]) to Ishigami and Furu (modern [[Tenri]]), reaching Miwa (modern [[Sakurai]]). Stretching approximately 35 km, its width is less than 2 meters. Along its route are historical landmarks such as [[Isonokami Shrine]], [[Ōmiwa Shrine]], and ancient burial mounds like [[Suijin's Mausoleum]] and [[Keikō's Mausoleum]].
The area's geography, characterized by wetlands in the Nara Basin, influenced the road's layout, hugging the foothills to avoid swamps{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.
== Landmarks and Sites Along the Route == * [[Isonokami Shrine]] * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] * [[Hase Temple]] * [[Suijin Mausoleum]] * [[Keikō Mausoleum]] * [[Kurozuka Kofun]] * [[Kashihara Shrine]] * [[Tenri University Sankokan Museum]]
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|author=Asai Kenji|title=The Dictionary of Roads|date=2001|publisher=Japanese Business Press|isbn=4-534-03315-X}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=Rom International (ed.)|title=Surprising Knowledge of Roads|date=2005|publisher=Kawade Shobo Shinsha|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}

The area's geography, characterized by wetlands in the Nara Basin, influenced the road's layout, hugging the foothills to avoid swamps{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.
== Landmarks and Sites Along the Route == * [[Isonokami Shrine]] * [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] * [[Hase Temple]] * [[Suijin Mausoleum]] * [[Keikō Mausoleum]] * [[Kurozuka Kofun]] * [[Kashihara Shrine]] * [[Tenri University Sankokan Museum]]

{{Unreferenced|date=June 8, 2012}} [[File:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-michi, near [[Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[Nara Prefecture]].]] [[File:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-michi]]

[[ja:山辺の道]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Yamanobe no Michi]] to [[Yamanobe-no-Michi]]

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=June 8, 2012}} [[File:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi near {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}'s {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}.]] [[File:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi]]
'''Yamanobe-no-Michi''' (山辺の道, {{small|Ancient Pronunciation}}: Yamanobe-no-Michi, {{small|Classical Writing}}: '''山辺道''', {{small|Old-Character Style}}: '''山邊道''') is one of {{ill|Japan's ancient roads|ja|日本の古道}} and a representative example of {{ill|Yamato's ancient roads|ja|大和の古道}}. It refers to a road that traversed the foothills of ancient {{ill|Yamato|ja|大和}} (a region known as Yamanobe, the origin of {{ill|Yamanobe District|ja|山辺郡}}). It is recognized as the '''oldest road in Japanese history''' and as the '''oldest existing road in Japan'''{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.
The road runs from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Miwa|ja|三輪山}}, in the southeast of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, to the foothills of {{ill|Kasuga Mountain|ja|春日山}} in the northeast. It follows the eastern edge of the basin, skirting the foothills along the Kasuga Fault Escarpment in a north-south direction.
Though some local government authorities use the variant spelling "Yama-no-be no Michi" (山の辺の道), it does not adhere to historical or {{ill|toponymy|ja|地名学}} conventions. In contrast, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} uses the classical spelling "Yamanobe-michi" or "Yamabe-michi"{{note|Example: The Imperial Household Agency manages {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} such as {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, officially referred to as "Yamabe-michi Makari-no-Oka Kami-no-Misasagi" (山辺道勾岡上陵)}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} includes the following reference in the section about Emperor Sujin:
{{Quotation|The imperial mausoleum was constructed along the Yamabe-no-Michi.|Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin Chapter}}
This indicates that Yamanobe-no-Michi was established before Emperor Sujin's mausoleum was built.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} contains similar descriptions in its accounts of Emperors Sujin and Keikō. These descriptions further support the idea that Yamanobe-no-Michi had already been established during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (4th century CE){{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.
Archaeological findings suggest that it may have existed as early as the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, connecting the {{ill|Furu Ruins|ja|布留遺跡}} and {{ill|Makimuku Ruins|ja|纏向遺跡}}.
[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|A monument inscribed with a {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}} poem by {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂}}. Located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}.]] [[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社}} in Tenri City.]]
Stretching approximately 35 kilometers and measuring less than 2 meters wide, the road passes through key historical sites such as {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|ja|石上神宮}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}, and the {{ill|Imperial mausoleums of Emperor Sujin and Emperor Keikō|ja|天皇陵}}. These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.
=== Course ===
The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the {{ill|Tokai Nature Trail|ja|東海自然歩道}}. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}} and Ōmiwa Shrine in {{ill|Sakurai|ja|桜井市}}. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.
== Key Sites Along the Route ==
* {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|ja|石上神宮}} * {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} * {{ill|Long-tomb Kofun clusters|ja|古墳群}} * {{ill|Kasuga Mountain|ja|春日山}}
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Asai Kenji|title=Dictionary of Roads and Paths|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyo Shuppansha|date=2001|isbn=4-534-03315-X}} * {{Cite book|author=Rom International (ed.)|title=Knowledge of Roads and History|publisher=Kawade Shobō|date=2005|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}
[[Category:Oiginal pages]]
[[ja:山辺の道]]

[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|A monument inscribed with a {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}} poem by {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂}}. Located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}.]] [[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社}} in Tenri City.]]

These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.
=== Course ===
The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the {{ill|Tokai Nature Trail|ja|東海自然歩道}}. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}} and Ōmiwa Shrine in {{ill|Sakurai|ja|桜井市}}. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.
== Key Sites Along the Route ==
* {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|ja|石上神宮}} * {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} * {{ill|Long-tomb Kofun clusters|ja|古墳群}} * {{ill|Kasuga Mountain|ja|春日山}}

It refers to a road that traversed the foothills of ancient {{ill|Yamato|ja|大和}} (a region known as Yamanobe, the origin of {{ill|Yamanobe District|ja|山辺郡}}). It is recognized as the '''oldest road in Japanese history''' and as the '''oldest existing road in Japan'''{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.
The road runs from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Miwa|ja|三輪山}}, in the southeast of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, to the foothills of {{ill|Kasuga Mountain|ja|春日山}} in the northeast. It follows the eastern edge of the basin, skirting the foothills along the Kasuga Fault Escarpment in a north-south direction.
Though some local government authorities use the variant spelling "Yama-no-be no Michi" (山の辺の道), it does not adhere to historical or {{ill|toponymy|ja|地名学}} conventions. In contrast, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} uses the classical spelling "Yamanobe-michi" or "Yamabe-michi"{{note|Example: The Imperial Household Agency manages {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} such as {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, officially referred to as "Yamabe-michi Makari-no-Oka Kami-no-Misasagi" (山辺道勾岡上陵)}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} includes the following reference in the section about Emperor Sujin:
{{Quotation|The imperial mausoleum was constructed along the Yamabe-no-Michi.|Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin Chapter}}
This indicates that Yamanobe-no-Michi was established before Emperor Sujin's mausoleum was built.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} contains similar descriptions in its accounts of Emperors Sujin and Keikō. These descriptions further support the idea that Yamanobe-no-Michi had already been established during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (4th century CE){{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.
Archaeological findings suggest that it may have existed as early as the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, connecting the {{ill|Furu Ruins|ja|布留遺跡}} and {{ill|Makimuku Ruins|ja|纏向遺跡}}.
[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|A monument inscribed with a {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}} poem by {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂}}. Located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}.]] [[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社}} in Tenri City.]]
Stretching approximately 35 kilometers and measuring less than 2 meters wide, the road passes through key historical sites such as {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|ja|石上神宮}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}, and the {{ill|Imperial mausoleums of Emperor Sujin and Emperor Keikō|ja|天皇陵}}. These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.
=== Course ===
The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the {{ill|Tokai Nature Trail|ja|東海自然歩道}}. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}} and Ōmiwa Shrine in {{ill|Sakurai|ja|桜井市}}. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.
== Key Sites Along the Route ==
* {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|ja|石上神宮}} * {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} * {{ill|Long-tomb Kofun clusters|ja|古墳群}} * {{ill|Kasuga Mountain|ja|春日山}}
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Asai Kenji|title=Dictionary of Roads and Paths|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyo Shuppansha|date=2001|isbn=4-534-03315-X}} * {{Cite book|author=Rom International (ed.)|title=Knowledge of Roads and History|publisher=Kawade Shobō|date=2005|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}

{{Citation needed|date=June 8, 2012}} [[File:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi near {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}'s {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}.]] [[File:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi]]
'''Yamanobe-no-Michi''' (山辺の道, {{small|Ancient Pronunciation}}: Yamanobe-no-Michi, {{small|Classical Writing}}: '''山辺道''', {{small|Old-Character Style}}: '''山邊道''') is one of {{ill|Japan's ancient roads|ja|日本の古道}} and a representative example of {{ill|Yamato's ancient roads|ja|大和の古道}}. It refers to a road that traversed the foothills of ancient {{ill|Yamato|ja|大和}} (a region known as Yamanobe, the origin of {{ill|Yamanobe District|ja|山辺郡}}). It is recognized as the '''oldest road in Japanese history''' and as the '''oldest existing road in Japan'''{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.
The road runs from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Miwa|ja|三輪山}}, in the southeast of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, to the foothills of {{ill|Kasuga Mountain|ja|春日山}} in the northeast. It follows the eastern edge of the basin, skirting the foothills along the Kasuga Fault Escarpment in a north-south direction.
Though some local government authorities use the variant spelling "Yama-no-be no Michi" (山の辺の道), it does not adhere to historical or {{ill|toponymy|ja|地名学}} conventions. In contrast, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} uses the classical spelling "Yamanobe-michi" or "Yamabe-michi"{{efn|Example: The Imperial Household Agency manages {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} such as {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳}}, officially referred to as "Yamabe-michi Makari-no-Oka Kami-no-Misasagi" (山辺道勾岡上陵)}}.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} includes the following reference in the section about Emperor Sujin:
{{Quotation|The imperial mausoleum was constructed along the Yamabe-no-Michi.|Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin Chapter}}
This indicates that Yamanobe-no-Michi was established before Emperor Sujin's mausoleum was built.
Additionally, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} contains similar descriptions in its accounts of Emperors Sujin and Keikō. These descriptions further support the idea that Yamanobe-no-Michi had already been established during the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (4th century CE){{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.
Archaeological findings suggest that it may have existed as early as the late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}}, connecting the {{ill|Furu Ruins|ja|布留遺跡}} and {{ill|Makimuku Ruins|ja|纏向遺跡}}.
[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|A monument inscribed with a {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|万葉集}} poem by {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|ja|柿本人麻呂}}. Located in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}}.]] [[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社}} in Tenri City.]]
Stretching approximately 35 kilometers and measuring less than 2 meters wide, the road passes through key historical sites such as {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|ja|石上神宮}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}}, and the {{ill|Imperial mausoleums of Emperor Sujin and Emperor Keikō|ja|天皇陵}}. These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.
=== Course ===
The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the [[:en:Tōkai_Nature_Trail|Tōkai Nature Trail]]. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in {{ill|Tenri|ja|天理市}} and Ōmiwa Shrine in {{ill|Sakurai|ja|桜井市}}. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.
== Key Sites Along the Route ==
* {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|ja|石上神宮}} * {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} * {{ill|Kofun clusters|ja|古墳群}} * {{ill|Kasuga Mountain|ja|春日山}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/>
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Asai Kenji|title=Dictionary of Roads and Paths|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyo Shuppansha|date=2001|isbn=4-534-03315-X}} * {{Cite book|author=Rom International (ed.)|title=Knowledge of Roads and History|publisher=Kawade Shobō|date=2005|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2012年6月8日 (金) 14:19 (UTC)}} [[ファイル:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|山辺の道／[[奈良県]][[天理市]]付近。]] [[ファイル:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|山辺の道]] '''山辺の道'''（やまのべのみち、{{small|古代読み}}：やまのへのみち、{{small|古風な表記}}：'''山辺道'''、{{small|[[旧字]]表記}}：'''山邊道'''）は、[[日本]]の[[古道]]の代表的な一つ。[[大和の古道]]の一つ。古代[[大和]]の山辺（{{small|やまのへ}}。[[山辺郡]]の語源にあたる[[地域]]名）に通した[[道路|道]]である。'''[[日本の歴史|日本史]]上'''（[[記録]]上）'''最古の道'''{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=136}}、'''日本現存最古の道'''として知られる。 [[奈良盆地]]の東南にある[[三輪山]]の麓から東北部の[[春日山 (奈良県)|春日山]]の麓まで、[[盆地]]の東縁、春日断層崖下を山々の裾を縫うように南北に走る。 「'''山の辺の道'''」という異綴を当該地域の[[地方公共団体|地方自治体]]までもが用いているが、歴史や[[地名学]]を踏まえた表記ではない。一方で[[宮内庁]]は古来の「'''山辺道'''/'''山邊道'''」を用いる用例：[[行燈山古墳]]の宮内庁管理名称「山辺道勾岡上陵/山邊道勾岡上陵（やまのべのみちのまがりのおかのえのみささぎ）」。 == 概要 == 『[[日本書紀]]』では、[[崇神天皇]]の条に次のような記述がある。 {{枠の始まり|青}}《崇神天皇条 [[原文]]抜粋》葬于山邊道上陵　《[[原文#書き下し|書き下し文]]》{{Ruby|山辺道|やまのへのみち}}の{{Ruby|上|ほとり}}の{{Ruby|陵|みさざき}}御陵・陵を、[[上代日本語]]では「みさざき」と呼んだ。に{{Ruby|葬|さう}}す。　{{Cite web|和書|url=http://tsukudaosamu.com/pdf/shin_kodaishi_04.pdf |title=崇神天皇と夫餘 - 日本古代史の復元 |author= |date=2005年冬|format=PDF |work=日本古代史の復元 -佃収著作集- |publisher=佃収 |accessdate=2019-04-01 }}{{rp|page=117}} {{枠の終わり}} 文脈どおりに読めば山辺の道が[[崇神天皇#陵・霊廟|崇神天皇陵]]の造営以前にあったと解釈できる。 また、『[[古事記]]』には、崇神天皇の条と[[景行天皇]]の条に、それぞれ次のような記述がある。 {{枠の始まり|赤}}《崇神天皇条 原文抜粋》御陵在山邊道勾之崗上也　《書き下し文》{{Ruby|御陵|みさざき}}は{{Ruby|山辺道|やまのへのみち}}の{{Ruby|勾の岡|まがりのおか}}の{{Ruby|上|ほとり}}に{{Ruby|在|あ}}る{{Ruby|也|なり}}。　{{rp|page=117}}
《景行天皇条 原文抜粋》御陵在山邊道上　《書き下し文》御陵は山辺道の{{Ruby|上|ほとり}}に在り。 {{枠の終わり}} これらの記述を論拠として、山辺の道は、[[古墳時代]]の初期、[[4世紀]]の崇神天皇の時代には既に整備されていたと考えられている{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=136}}。もっとも、[[弥生時代]]後期には既に[[布留遺跡]]と[[纏向遺跡]]を結ぶ道として存在していたとも推測されている。 [[ファイル:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|山辺の道沿いに建つ、[[柿本人麻呂]]の[[万葉集|万葉]][[歌碑]] ---- 歌は「衾道乎 引手乃山尓 妹乎置而 山徑徃者 生跡毛無（[[原文#書き下し|書き下し文]]：{{Ruby|衾道|ふすまぢ}}を {{Ruby|引手の山|ひきてのやま}}に {{Ruby|妹|いも}}を{{Ruby|置|お}}きて {{Ruby|山路|やまぢ}}を{{Ruby|行|ゆ}}けば {{Ruby|生|い}}けりともなし）」──『万葉集』巻2・212。碑文の[[揮毫]]は[[犬養孝]]。所在地は[[天理市]]中山町にある中山寺跡の前万葉歌碑（柿本人麻呂）（{{googlemap|万葉歌碑（柿本人麻呂）}}）。]] [[ファイル:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|影媛道行歌の歌碑／天理市櫟本町（旧・[[添上郡]][[櫟本町]]）に鎮座する[[和爾下神社 (天理市)|和爾下神社]]の参道脇に建つ。]] [[大和国]]曾布（そふ）/層富（そほ）（のちの[[奈良]]〈添郡［そふのこおり］東部、のちの[[添上郡]]〉、現在の[[奈良市]]中核）から石上・布留（[[式上郡]]の石上・布留、現・[[天理市]]石神町・布留町）を経て三輪（式上郡三輪、現・[[桜井市]]三輪、[[三輪町 (奈良県)|''cf.'']] ）に通じていたとみられ、その全長は約35[[キロメートル]]、幅は2[[メートル]]足らずの小道であるが、沿道には[[石上神宮]]、[[大神神社]]、[[長岳寺]]、[[崇神天皇陵]]、[[景行天皇陵]]、[[金谷石仏]]などの多くの寺社や古墳群があり、この地に権力を握る古代国家の中枢があったことや、文化交流の重要な幹線道路であったことをうかがわせている{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|浅井建爾|2015|p=107}}。古墳時代の奈良盆地は沼地や湿地が多く、これを避けて山林、集落、田畑の間を縫うように山裾に沿ってつくられたため、道は曲がりくねっている{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=136}}。 『[[日本書紀]]』巻第16 [[武烈天皇]]即位前紀には、政治的謀略によって乃楽山（{{small|ならやま}}、[[平城山丘陵]]、奈良山）で討たれた[[平群鮪]]を追う[[物部影媛]]（[[物部麁鹿火]]の娘）の悲しみを詠んだという歌が収められている。[[通称]]「影媛道行歌（{{small|かげひめ みちゆきのうた}}）」「影媛歌（{{small|かげひめのうた}}）」などと呼ばれるものである。 {{Quotation|《[[原文]]》　石上布留過、薦枕高橋過、物多大宅過、春日春日過、妻隱小佐保過、玉笥飯盛、玉碗水盛、泣沾行影媛。
《[[原文#書き下し|書き下し文]]》　{{Ruby|石上|いすのかみ}} {{Ruby|布留|ふる}}を過ぎて、{{Ruby|薦枕|こもまくら}} {{Ruby|高橋|たかはし}}過ぎ、{{Ruby|物多|ものさは}}に{{Ruby|大宅|おほやけ}}過ぎ、{{Ruby|春日|はるひ}} {{Ruby|春日|かすが}}を過ぎ、{{Ruby|妻隠|つまごも}}る{{Ruby|小佐保|をさほ}}を過ぎ、{{Ruby|玉笥|たまけ}}には{{Ruby|飯|いひ}}さへ{{Ruby|盛|も}}り、{{Ruby|玉碗|たまもひ}}に{{Ruby|水|みづ}}さへ盛り、泣き{{Ruby|沾|そぼ}}ち{{Ruby|行|ゆ}}くも{{Ruby|影媛|かげひめ}}あはれ。}} ここで詠い込まれている、石上（いしのかみ）（布留の一角）－布留（布留川流域一帯。[[物部氏]]の拠点）－高橋－大宅（おおやけ）（[[和珥氏]]支族・[[大宅氏]]の本拠。現・奈良市白毫寺町あたり）－春日（はるひ）－春日（かすが）（和珥氏支族・[[春日氏]]の本拠。現在の[[春日大社]][[境内]]）－小佐保（奈良市法連の[[佐保川]]流域の一角）を経由して乃楽山へ到る道筋は、山辺道の延長であろうという[[角川書店]]『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 29 奈良県』 1138-1139頁[[平凡社]]『日本歴史地名大系 30 奈良県の地名』 28–29頁。時代の経過とともに奈良盆地の湿地や沼地が乾燥して、平地部に直線的な道路が開かれるようになると、山辺の道を利用する人々は減少していき、しだいに西側の[[上街道 (上ツ道)|上ツ道]]が多く用いられるようになったと考えられている{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=137}}。 三輪山近くにあった[[海柘榴市 (大和国)|海石榴市]]（つばいち）は、日本最古の市が立った所といわれている{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001|p=82}}。南部に古道の痕跡や景観が残り、20世紀末期および21世紀初期において、一般的に[[ハイキング]]コースとして親しまれるのは、天理市の[[石上神宮]]から桜井市の[[大神神社]]付近までの約15kmの行程で、その多くは[[東海自然歩道]]となっている『大和を歩く-ひとあじちがう歴史地理探訪』 178-180頁。また、石上神宮から北部にも山辺の道は続いていたと考えられているが、長い歳月で風化が進んでしまっていることから、今日においてもその道筋の詳細はわかっていない{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=137}}。 田畑の間を抜ける際にはその眼下に奈良盆地が大きく開けており、[[生駒山]]や[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]、そして[[大和葛城山|葛城]]・[[金剛山 (金剛山地)|金剛]]の連嶺を背景にした[[大和三山]]なども遠望できる。 === 起点 === 現在のその道の起点は、海石榴市（つばいち、椿市:つばきのいち）である。古代には、海石榴市の八十（ヤソ）の衢（ちまた）と称されたところで、桜井市粟殿（おおどの）を中心とした地域であった。[[平安時代]]中期の[[延長 (元号)|延長]]4年（[[926年]]）には椿市観音堂付近が起点の地になった。 === 海石榴市、椿市 === この市は、政治の中心が主として[[奈良盆地]]の東南部にあった頃、定期的に市が立って栄えた。北へたどる山辺の道の起点であり、そこに[[初瀬街道]]がT字形に合し、さらに[[飛鳥]]からの山田の道、磐余の道などの主要な街道が集まり『ふるさとの文化遺産 郷土資料事典 29 奈良県』 143頁、また[[初瀬川 (奈良県)|初瀬川]]を下り大和川に出る水運の河港もでき、水陸交通などの要衝の土地であった。三輪山の南、今の[[桜井市]]金屋付近である。 [[推古天皇]]16年[[8月 (旧暦)|8月]]の条に「唐の客を海石榴市の衢に迎ふ」とある。路傍に「海石榴市観音道」の石の道標があり、少し離れたところに「海石榴市観音堂」がある。 {{Quotation|{{Ruby|海石榴市|つばいち}}の {{Ruby|八十|やそ}}の{{Ruby|衢|ちまた}}に 立ちならし 結びし紐を 解かまく惜しも　|作者不詳|『[[万葉集]]』巻12・2951}} === 道程 === 金屋の集落を後にして三輪山の山麓を北へ行くと三輪山の神である[[大物主]]を[[祭神]]とする[[大神神社]]につく{{sfn|浅井建爾|2015|p=107}}。大神神社は、日本最古の神社で大和国の一宮である{{sfn|浅井建爾|2015|p=107}}。さらに北の沿道には、[[茅原大墓古墳]]・[[景行天皇陵]]・[[崇神天皇陵]]・[[櫛山古墳]]・[[西殿古墳]]・[[東乗鞍古墳]]・[[西乗鞍古墳]]などの古墳群がある{{sfn|浅井建爾|2015|p=107}}。また、茅原大墓古墳のすぐ西側に、[[倭迹迹日百襲姫命]]の墓とされる[[箸墓古墳]]があり、古代・[[邪馬台国]]を治めていた[[卑弥呼]]の墓とする説もある{{sfn|浅井建爾|2015|p=107}}。[[古墳時代]]にはいると山麓地帯には墳丘長200メートルを超える巨大古墳が造られた。『[[古事記]]』には、「山辺道の勾の岡{{small|（まがりのおか）}}」の近辺に[[崇神天皇]]の[[行燈山古墳|陵]]（墳丘長242メートル）が、山辺道の近辺に[[景行天皇]]の[[渋谷向山古墳|陵]]（310メートル）があると記している。初期大和政権がこの地に誕生したと考えられている。 == 沿線の名所・旧跡 == * [[石上神宮]] * [[内山永久寺]]跡 * [[大和神社]] * [[西山塚古墳]] * [[長岳寺]] * 伝[[崇神天皇]]陵 * [[黒塚古墳]] * [[櫛山古墳]] * [[大和天神山古墳|天神山古墳]] * [[渋谷向山古墳|伝景行天皇陵]] * [[箸墓古墳]] * [[大神神社#本社近辺|檜原神社]] * [[穴師坐兵主神社]] * [[夜都伎神社]] * [[三輪山]] * [[大神神社]] * [[竹之内環濠集落]] * [[萱生環濠集落]] * [[磯城瑞籬宮]] == 沿線の施設 == * [[天理市立黒塚古墳展示館]] * [[なら歴史芸術文化村]] * [[トレイル青垣]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group=*}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=浅井建爾 |edition= 初版|date=2001-11-10 |title=道と路がわかる辞典 |publisher=[[日本実業出版社]] |isbn=4-534-03315-X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book |和書 |author=浅井建爾 |edition= 初版|date=2015-10-10 |title=日本の道路がわかる辞典 |publisher=日本実業出版社 |isbn=978-4-534-05318-3 |ref=harv}} * [[奈良地理学会]]編『大和を歩く-ひとあじちがう歴史地理探訪』[[奈良新聞社]] 2000年 ISBN 4-88856-031-5 * {{Cite book |和書 |author=ロム・インターナショナル（編） |date=2005-02-01 |title=道路地図 びっくり!博学知識 |publisher=[[河出書房新社]] |series=KAWADE夢文庫|isbn=4-309-49566-4|ref=harv}} * ワークス編『ふるさとの文化遺産 郷土資料事典 29 奈良県』[[人文社]] 1997年 == 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Yamanobe-no-michi|山辺の道}} * [[大和の古道]] * [[大和青垣国定公園]] * [[桜井線]]（JR西日本。愛称・[[万葉集#万葉集に由来する名前|万葉まほろば線]]。山辺の道に並行し、[[大和三山]]や[[藤原京]]付近などを通過することからこの愛称が制定された） *[[国道169号]]（桜井市内はほぼ平行している） * revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[山辺の道]] to [[Yamanobe-no-Michi]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2012年6月8日 (金) 14:19 (UTC)}} [[ファイル:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|山辺の道／[[奈良県]][[天理市]]付近。]] [[ファイル:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|山辺の道]] '''山辺の道'''（やまのべのみち、{{small|古代読み}}：やまのへのみち、{{small|古風な表記}}：'''山辺道'''、{{small|[[旧字]]表記}}：'''山邊道'''）は、[[日本]]の[[古道]]の代表的な一つ。[[大和の古道]]の一つ。古代[[大和]]の山辺（{{small|やまのへ}}。[[山辺郡]]の語源にあたる[[地域]]名）に通した[[道路|道]]である。'''[[日本の歴史|日本史]]上'''（[[記録]]上）'''最古の道'''{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=136}}、'''日本現存最古の道'''として知られる。 [[奈良盆地]]の東南にある[[三輪山]]の麓から東北部の[[春日山 (奈良県)|春日山]]の麓まで、[[盆地]]の東縁、春日断層崖下を山々の裾を縫うように南北に走る。 「'''山の辺の道'''」という異綴を当該地域の[[地方公共団体|地方自治体]]までもが用いているが、歴史や[[地名学]]を踏まえた表記ではない。一方で[[宮内庁]]は古来の「'''山辺道'''/'''山邊道'''」を用いる用例：[[行燈山古墳]]の宮内庁管理名称「山辺道勾岡上陵/山邊道勾岡上陵（やまのべのみちのまがりのおかのえのみささぎ）」。 == 概要 == 『[[日本書紀]]』では、[[崇神天皇]]の条に次のような記述がある。 {{枠の始まり|青}}《崇神天皇条 [[原文]]抜粋》葬于山邊道上陵　《[[原文#書き下し|書き下し文]]》{{Ruby|山辺道|やまのへのみち}}の{{Ruby|上|ほとり}}の{{Ruby|陵|みさざき}}御陵・陵を、[[上代日本語]]では「みさざき」と呼んだ。に{{Ruby|葬|さう}}す。　{{Cite web|和書|url=http://tsukudaosamu.com/pdf/shin_kodaishi_04.pdf |title=崇神天皇と夫餘 - 日本古代史の復元 |author= |date=2005年冬|format=PDF |work=日本古代史の復元 -佃収著作集- |publisher=佃収 |accessdate=2019-04-01 }}{{rp|page=117}} {{枠の終わり}} 文脈どおりに読めば山辺の道が[[崇神天皇#陵・霊廟|崇神天皇陵]]の造営以前にあったと解釈できる。 また、『[[古事記]]』には、崇神天皇の条と[[景行天皇]]の条に、それぞれ次のような記述がある。 {{枠の始まり|赤}}《崇神天皇条 原文抜粋》御陵在山邊道勾之崗上也　《書き下し文》{{Ruby|御陵|みさざき}}は{{Ruby|山辺道|やまのへのみち}}の{{Ruby|勾の岡|まがりのおか}}の{{Ruby|上|ほとり}}に{{Ruby|在|あ}}る{{Ruby|也|なり}}。　{{rp|page=117}}
《景行天皇条 原文抜粋》御陵在山邊道上　《書き下し文》御陵は山辺道の{{Ruby|上|ほとり}}に在り。 {{枠の終わり}} これらの記述を論拠として、山辺の道は、[[古墳時代]]の初期、[[4世紀]]の崇神天皇の時代には既に整備されていたと考えられている{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=136}}。もっとも、[[弥生時代]]後期には既に[[布留遺跡]]と[[纏向遺跡]]を結ぶ道として存在していたとも推測されている。 [[ファイル:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|山辺の道沿いに建つ、[[柿本人麻呂]]の[[万葉集|万葉]][[歌碑]] ---- 歌は「衾道乎 引手乃山尓 妹乎置而 山徑徃者 生跡毛無（[[原文#書き下し|書き下し文]]：{{Ruby|衾道|ふすまぢ}}を {{Ruby|引手の山|ひきてのやま}}に {{Ruby|妹|いも}}を{{Ruby|置|お}}きて {{Ruby|山路|やまぢ}}を{{Ruby|行|ゆ}}けば {{Ruby|生|い}}けりともなし）」──『万葉集』巻2・212。碑文の[[揮毫]]は[[犬養孝]]。所在地は[[天理市]]中山町にある中山寺跡の前万葉歌碑（柿本人麻呂）（{{googlemap|万葉歌碑（柿本人麻呂）}}）。]] [[ファイル:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|影媛道行歌の歌碑／天理市櫟本町（旧・[[添上郡]][[櫟本町]]）に鎮座する[[和爾下神社 (天理市)|和爾下神社]]の参道脇に建つ。]] [[大和国]]曾布（そふ）/層富（そほ）（のちの[[奈良]]〈添郡［そふのこおり］東部、のちの[[添上郡]]〉、現在の[[奈良市]]中核）から石上・布留（[[式上郡]]の石上・布留、現・[[天理市]]石神町・布留町）を経て三輪（式上郡三輪、現・[[桜井市]]三輪、[[三輪町 (奈良県)|''cf.'']] ）に通じていたとみられ、その全長は約35[[キロメートル]]、幅は2[[メートル]]足らずの小道であるが、沿道には[[石上神宮]]、[[大神神社]]、[[長岳寺]]、[[崇神天皇陵]]、[[景行天皇陵]]、[[金谷石仏]]などの多くの寺社や古墳群があり、この地に権力を握る古代国家の中枢があったことや、文化交流の重要な幹線道路であったことをうかがわせている{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|浅井建爾|2015|p=107}}。古墳時代の奈良盆地は沼地や湿地が多く、これを避けて山林、集落、田畑の間を縫うように山裾に沿ってつくられたため、道は曲がりくねっている{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=136}}。 『[[日本書紀]]』巻第16 [[武烈天皇]]即位前紀には、政治的謀略によって乃楽山（{{small|ならやま}}、[[平城山丘陵]]、奈良山）で討たれた[[平群鮪]]を追う[[物部影媛]]（[[物部麁鹿火]]の娘）の悲しみを詠んだという歌が収められている。[[通称]]「影媛道行歌（{{small|かげひめ みちゆきのうた}}）」「影媛歌（{{small|かげひめのうた}}）」などと呼ばれるものである。 {{Quotation|《[[原文]]》　石上布留過、薦枕高橋過、物多大宅過、春日春日過、妻隱小佐保過、玉笥飯盛、玉碗水盛、泣沾行影媛。
《[[原文#書き下し|書き下し文]]》　{{Ruby|石上|いすのかみ}} {{Ruby|布留|ふる}}を過ぎて、{{Ruby|薦枕|こもまくら}} {{Ruby|高橋|たかはし}}過ぎ、{{Ruby|物多|ものさは}}に{{Ruby|大宅|おほやけ}}過ぎ、{{Ruby|春日|はるひ}} {{Ruby|春日|かすが}}を過ぎ、{{Ruby|妻隠|つまごも}}る{{Ruby|小佐保|をさほ}}を過ぎ、{{Ruby|玉笥|たまけ}}には{{Ruby|飯|いひ}}さへ{{Ruby|盛|も}}り、{{Ruby|玉碗|たまもひ}}に{{Ruby|水|みづ}}さへ盛り、泣き{{Ruby|沾|そぼ}}ち{{Ruby|行|ゆ}}くも{{Ruby|影媛|かげひめ}}あはれ。}} ここで詠い込まれている、石上（いしのかみ）（布留の一角）－布留（布留川流域一帯。[[物部氏]]の拠点）－高橋－大宅（おおやけ）（[[和珥氏]]支族・[[大宅氏]]の本拠。現・奈良市白毫寺町あたり）－春日（はるひ）－春日（かすが）（和珥氏支族・[[春日氏]]の本拠。現在の[[春日大社]][[境内]]）－小佐保（奈良市法連の[[佐保川]]流域の一角）を経由して乃楽山へ到る道筋は、山辺道の延長であろうという[[角川書店]]『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 29 奈良県』 1138-1139頁[[平凡社]]『日本歴史地名大系 30 奈良県の地名』 28–29頁。時代の経過とともに奈良盆地の湿地や沼地が乾燥して、平地部に直線的な道路が開かれるようになると、山辺の道を利用する人々は減少していき、しだいに西側の[[上街道 (上ツ道)|上ツ道]]が多く用いられるようになったと考えられている{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=137}}。 三輪山近くにあった[[海柘榴市 (大和国)|海石榴市]]（つばいち）は、日本最古の市が立った所といわれている{{sfn|浅井建爾|2001|p=82}}。南部に古道の痕跡や景観が残り、20世紀末期および21世紀初期において、一般的に[[ハイキング]]コースとして親しまれるのは、天理市の[[石上神宮]]から桜井市の[[大神神社]]付近までの約15kmの行程で、その多くは[[東海自然歩道]]となっている『大和を歩く-ひとあじちがう歴史地理探訪』 178-180頁。また、石上神宮から北部にも山辺の道は続いていたと考えられているが、長い歳月で風化が進んでしまっていることから、今日においてもその道筋の詳細はわかっていない{{sfn|ロム・インターナショナル（編）|2005|p=137}}。 田畑の間を抜ける際にはその眼下に奈良盆地が大きく開けており、[[生駒山]]や[[二上山 (奈良県・大阪府)|二上山]]、そして[[大和葛城山|葛城]]・[[金剛山 (金剛山地)|金剛]]の連嶺を背景にした[[大和三山]]なども遠望できる。 === 起点 === 現在のその道の起点は、海石榴市（つばいち、椿市:つばきのいち）である。古代には、海石榴市の八十（ヤソ）の衢（ちまた）と称されたところで、桜井市粟殿（おおどの）を中心とした地域であった。[[平安時代]]中期の[[延長 (元号)|延長]]4年（[[926年]]）には椿市観音堂付近が起点の地になった。 === 海石榴市、椿市 === この市は、政治の中心が主として[[奈良盆地]]の東南部にあった頃、定期的に市が立って栄えた。北へたどる山辺の道の起点であり、そこに[[初瀬街道]]がT字形に合し、さらに[[飛鳥]]からの山田の道、磐余の道などの主要な街道が集まり『ふるさとの文化遺産 郷土資料事典 29 奈良県』 143頁、また[[初瀬川 (奈良県)|初瀬川]]を下り大和川に出る水運の河港もでき、水陸交通などの要衝の土地であった。三輪山の南、今の[[桜井市]]金屋付近である。 [[推古天皇]]16年[[8月 (旧暦)|8月]]の条に「唐の客を海石榴市の衢に迎ふ」とある。路傍に「海石榴市観音道」の石の道標があり、少し離れたところに「海石榴市観音堂」がある。 {{Quotation|{{Ruby|海石榴市|つばいち}}の {{Ruby|八十|やそ}}の{{Ruby|衢|ちまた}}に 立ちならし 結びし紐を 解かまく惜しも　|作者不詳|『[[万葉集]]』巻12・2951}} === 道程 === 金屋の集落を後にして三輪山の山麓を北へ行くと三輪山の神である[[大物主]]を[[祭神]]とする[[大神神社]]につく{{sfn|浅井建爾|2015|p=107}}。大神神社は、日本最古の神社で大和国の一宮である{{sfn|浅井建爾|2015|p=107}}。さらに北の沿道には、[[茅原大墓古墳]]・[[景行天皇陵]]・[[崇神天皇陵]]・[[櫛山古墳]]・[[西殿古墳]]・[[東乗鞍古墳]]・[[西乗鞍古墳]]などの古墳群がある{{sfn|浅井建爾|2015|p=107}}。また、茅原大墓古墳のすぐ西側に、[[倭迹迹日百襲姫命]]の墓とされる[[箸墓古墳]]があり、古代・[[邪馬台国]]を治めていた[[卑弥呼]]の墓とする説もある{{sfn|浅井建爾|2015|p=107}}。[[古墳時代]]にはいると山麓地帯には墳丘長200メートルを超える巨大古墳が造られた。『[[古事記]]』には、「山辺道の勾の岡{{small|（まがりのおか）}}」の近辺に[[崇神天皇]]の[[行燈山古墳|陵]]（墳丘長242メートル）が、山辺道の近辺に[[景行天皇]]の[[渋谷向山古墳|陵]]（310メートル）があると記している。初期大和政権がこの地に誕生したと考えられている。 == 沿線の名所・旧跡 == * [[石上神宮]] * [[内山永久寺]]跡 * [[大和神社]] * [[西山塚古墳]] * [[長岳寺]] * 伝[[崇神天皇]]陵 * [[黒塚古墳]] * [[櫛山古墳]] * [[大和天神山古墳|天神山古墳]] * [[渋谷向山古墳|伝景行天皇陵]] * [[箸墓古墳]] * [[大神神社#本社近辺|檜原神社]] * [[穴師坐兵主神社]] * [[夜都伎神社]] * [[三輪山]] * [[大神神社]] * [[竹之内環濠集落]] * [[萱生環濠集落]] * [[磯城瑞籬宮]] == 沿線の施設 == * [[天理市立黒塚古墳展示館]] * [[なら歴史芸術文化村]] * [[トレイル青垣]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group=*}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book |和書 |author=浅井建爾 |edition= 初版|date=2001-11-10 |title=道と路がわかる辞典 |publisher=[[日本実業出版社]] |isbn=4-534-03315-X |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book |和書 |author=浅井建爾 |edition= 初版|date=2015-10-10 |title=日本の道路がわかる辞典 |publisher=日本実業出版社 |isbn=978-4-534-05318-3 |ref=harv}} * [[奈良地理学会]]編『大和を歩く-ひとあじちがう歴史地理探訪』[[奈良新聞社]] 2000年 ISBN 4-88856-031-5 * {{Cite book |和書 |author=ロム・インターナショナル（編） |date=2005-02-01 |title=道路地図 びっくり!博学知識 |publisher=[[河出書房新社]] |series=KAWADE夢文庫|isbn=4-309-49566-4|ref=harv}} * ワークス編『ふるさとの文化遺産 郷土資料事典 29 奈良県』[[人文社]] 1997年 == 関連項目 == {{Commons|Category:Yamanobe-no-michi|山辺の道}} * [[大和の古道]] * [[大和青垣国定公園]] * [[桜井線]]（JR西日本。愛称・[[万葉集#万葉集に由来する名前|万葉まほろば線]]。山辺の道に並行し、[[大和三山]]や[[藤原京]]付近などを通過することからこの愛称が制定された） *[[国道169号]]（桜井市内はほぼ平行している） * revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山辺の道]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11470977]]
Wikidata name: Yamanobe-no-michi
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Citation needed|date=June 8, 2012}}
{{Expand Japanese}}
[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi near [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi]]

'''Yamanobe-no-Michi''' (山辺の道, {{small|Ancient Pronunciation}}: Yamanobe-no-Michi, {{small|Classical Writing}}: '''山辺道''', {{small|Old-Character Style}}: '''山邊道''') is one of {{ill|Japan's ancient roads|ja|日本の古道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's ancient roads|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a representative example of {{ill|Yamato's ancient roads|ja|大和の古道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato's ancient roads|qq=|1=Yamato's ancient roads|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433382}}. It refers to a road that traversed the foothills of ancient {{ill|Yamato|ja|大和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato|qq=|1=Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13417196}} (a region known as Yamanobe, the origin of [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]]). It is recognized as the '''oldest road in Japanese history''' and as the '''oldest existing road in Japan'''{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

The road runs from the foothills of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]], in the southeast of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], to the foothills of [[:en:Kasugayama|Kasuga Mountain]] in the northeast. It follows the eastern edge of the basin, skirting the foothills along the Kasuga Fault Escarpment in a north-south direction.

Though some local government authorities use the variant spelling "Yama-no-be no Michi" (山の辺の道), it does not adhere to historical or [[:en:Toponymy|toponymy]] conventions. In contrast, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] uses the classical spelling "Yamanobe-michi" or "Yamabe-michi"{{efn|Example: The Imperial Household Agency manages [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] such as {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, officially referred to as "Yamabe-michi Makari-no-Oka Kami-no-Misasagi" (山辺道勾岡上陵)}}.

== Overview ==

The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] includes the following reference in the section about Emperor Sujin:

{{Quotation|The imperial mausoleum was constructed along the Yamabe-no-Michi.|Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin Chapter}}

This indicates that Yamanobe-no-Michi was established before Emperor Sujin's mausoleum was built.

Additionally, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] contains similar descriptions in its accounts of Emperors Sujin and Keikō. These descriptions further support the idea that Yamanobe-no-Michi had already been established during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century CE){{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

Archaeological findings suggest that it may have existed as early as the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], connecting the {{ill|Furu Ruins|ja|布留遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furu Ruins|qq=}} and {{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纏向遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku ruins|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向遺跡}}.

[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|A monument inscribed with a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]] poem by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]]. Located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|lt=Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社_(天理市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|qq=|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282589}} in Tenri City.]]

Stretching approximately 35 kilometers and measuring less than 2 meters wide, the road passes through key historical sites such as [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]], [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]], and the [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]. These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.

=== Course ===

The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the [[:en:Tōkai_Nature_Trail|Tōkai Nature Trail]]. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]] and Ōmiwa Shrine in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]]. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.

Wikidata name: Yamanobe-no-michi
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Citation needed|date=June 8, 2012}}
{{Expand Japanese}}
[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi near [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi]]

'''Yamanobe-no-Michi''' (山辺の道, {{small|Ancient Pronunciation}}: Yamanobe-no-Michi, {{small|Classical Writing}}: '''山辺道''', {{small|Old-Character Style}}: '''山邊道''') is one of {{ill|Japan's ancient roads|ja|日本の古道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's ancient roads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a representative example of {{ill|Yamato's ancient roads|ja|大和の古道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato's ancient roads|1=Yamato's ancient roads|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433382}}. It refers to a road that traversed the foothills of ancient {{ill|Yamato|ja|大和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato|1=Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13417196}} (a region known as Yamanobe, the origin of [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]]). It is recognized as the '''oldest road in Japanese history''' and as the '''oldest existing road in Japan'''{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

The road runs from the foothills of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]], in the southeast of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], to the foothills of [[:en:Kasugayama|Kasuga Mountain]] in the northeast. It follows the eastern edge of the basin, skirting the foothills along the Kasuga Fault Escarpment in a north-south direction.

Though some local government authorities use the variant spelling "Yama-no-be no Michi" (山の辺の道), it does not adhere to historical or [[:en:Toponymy|toponymy]] conventions. In contrast, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] uses the classical spelling "Yamanobe-michi" or "Yamabe-michi"{{efn|Example: The Imperial Household Agency manages [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] such as {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, officially referred to as "Yamabe-michi Makari-no-Oka Kami-no-Misasagi" (山辺道勾岡上陵)}}.

== Overview ==

The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] includes the following reference in the section about Emperor Sujin:

{{Quotation|The imperial mausoleum was constructed along the Yamabe-no-Michi.|Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin Chapter}}

This indicates that Yamanobe-no-Michi was established before Emperor Sujin's mausoleum was built.

Additionally, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] contains similar descriptions in its accounts of Emperors Sujin and Keikō. These descriptions further support the idea that Yamanobe-no-Michi had already been established during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century CE){{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

Archaeological findings suggest that it may have existed as early as the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], connecting the {{ill|Furu Ruins|ja|布留遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furu Ruins|qq=}} and {{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纏向遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku ruins|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向遺跡}}.

[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|A monument inscribed with a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]] poem by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]]. Located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|lt=Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社_(天理市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282589}} in Tenri City.]]

Stretching approximately 35 kilometers and measuring less than 2 meters wide, the road passes through key historical sites such as [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]], [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]], and the [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]. These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.

=== Course ===

The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the [[:en:Tōkai_Nature_Trail|Tōkai Nature Trail]]. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]] and Ōmiwa Shrine in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]]. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Citation needed|date=June 8, 2012}}
{{Expand Japanese}}
[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi near [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi]]

'''Yamanobe-no-Michi''' (山辺の道, {{small|Ancient Pronunciation}}: Yamanobe-no-Michi, {{small|Classical Writing}}: '''山辺道''', {{small|Old-Character Style}}: '''山邊道''') is one of {{ill|Japan's ancient roads|ja|日本の古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's ancient roads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a representative example of {{ill|Yamato's ancient roads|ja|大和の古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato's ancient roads|1=Yamato's ancient roads|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433382}}. It refers to a road that traversed the foothills of ancient {{ill|Yamato|ja|大和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato|1=Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13417196}} (a region known as Yamanobe, the origin of [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]]). It is recognized as the '''oldest road in Japanese history''' and as the '''oldest existing road in Japan'''{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

The road runs from the foothills of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]], in the southeast of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], to the foothills of [[:en:Kasugayama|Kasuga Mountain]] in the northeast. It follows the eastern edge of the basin, skirting the foothills along the Kasuga Fault Escarpment in a north-south direction.

Though some local government authorities use the variant spelling "Yama-no-be no Michi" (山の辺の道), it does not adhere to historical or [[:en:Toponymy|toponymy]] conventions. In contrast, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] uses the classical spelling "Yamanobe-michi" or "Yamabe-michi"{{efn|Example: The Imperial Household Agency manages [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] such as {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, officially referred to as "Yamabe-michi Makari-no-Oka Kami-no-Misasagi" (山辺道勾岡上陵)}}.

== Overview ==

The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] includes the following reference in the section about Emperor Sujin:

{{Quotation|The imperial mausoleum was constructed along the Yamabe-no-Michi.|Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin Chapter}}

This indicates that Yamanobe-no-Michi was established before Emperor Sujin's mausoleum was built.

Additionally, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] contains similar descriptions in its accounts of Emperors Sujin and Keikō. These descriptions further support the idea that Yamanobe-no-Michi had already been established during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century CE){{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

Archaeological findings suggest that it may have existed as early as the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], connecting the {{ill|Furu Ruins|ja|布留遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furu Ruins|qq=}} and {{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纏向遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku ruins|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向遺跡}}.

[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|A monument inscribed with a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]] poem by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]]. Located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|lt=Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社_(天理市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282589}} in Tenri City.]]

Stretching approximately 35 kilometers and measuring less than 2 meters wide, the road passes through key historical sites such as [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]], [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]], and the [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]. These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.

=== Course ===

The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the [[:en:Tōkai_Nature_Trail|Tōkai Nature Trail]]. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]] and Ōmiwa Shrine in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]]. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.

* [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Yamato Shrine]]
* {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qq=}}
* [[:en:Chōgaku-ji|Chōgaku-ji]]
* {{ill|Tomb of Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Emperor Sujin|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=崇神天皇|1=Sujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q333530}}

* {{ill|Anashi Izasu Hyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashi Izasu Hyozu Shrine|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}
* {{ill|Yatogi Shrine|ja|夜都伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatogi Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=夜都岐神社|1=Yatsugi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431328}}
* [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]]
* [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山辺の道]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11470977]]
Wikidata name: Yamanobe-no-michi
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Citation needed|date=June 8, 2012}}
{{Expand Japanese}}
[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi near [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi]]

'''Yamanobe-no-Michi''' (山辺の道, {{small|Ancient Pronunciation}}: Yamanobe-no-Michi, {{small|Classical Writing}}: '''山辺道''', {{small|Old-Character Style}}: '''山邊道''') is one of {{ill|Japan's ancient roads|ja|日本の古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's ancient roads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a representative example of {{ill|Yamato's ancient roads|ja|大和の古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato's ancient roads|1=Yamato's ancient roads|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433382}}. It refers to a road that traversed the foothills of ancient {{ill|Yamato|ja|大和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato|1=Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13417196}} (a region known as Yamanobe, the origin of [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]]). It is recognized as the '''oldest road in Japanese history''' and as the '''oldest existing road in Japan'''{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

The road runs from the foothills of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]], in the southeast of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], to the foothills of [[:en:Kasugayama|Kasuga Mountain]] in the northeast. It follows the eastern edge of the basin, skirting the foothills along the Kasuga Fault Escarpment in a north-south direction.

Though some local government authorities use the variant spelling "Yama-no-be no Michi" (山の辺の道), it does not adhere to historical or [[:en:Toponymy|toponymy]] conventions. In contrast, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] uses the classical spelling "Yamanobe-michi" or "Yamabe-michi"{{efn|Example: The Imperial Household Agency manages [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] such as {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, officially referred to as "Yamabe-michi Makari-no-Oka Kami-no-Misasagi" (山辺道勾岡上陵)}}.

== Overview ==

The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] includes the following reference in the section about Emperor Sujin:

{{Quotation|The imperial mausoleum was constructed along the Yamabe-no-Michi.|Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin Chapter}}

This indicates that Yamanobe-no-Michi was established before Emperor Sujin's mausoleum was built.

Additionally, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] contains similar descriptions in its accounts of Emperors Sujin and Keikō. These descriptions further support the idea that Yamanobe-no-Michi had already been established during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century CE){{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

Archaeological findings suggest that it may have existed as early as the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], connecting the {{ill|Furu Ruins|ja|布留遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furu Ruins|qq=}} and {{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纏向遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku ruins|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向遺跡}}.

[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|A monument inscribed with a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]] poem by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]]. Located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|lt=Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社_(天理市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282589}} in Tenri City.]]

Stretching approximately 35 kilometers and measuring less than 2 meters wide, the road passes through key historical sites such as [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]], [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]], and the [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]. These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.

=== Course ===

The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the [[:en:Tōkai_Nature_Trail|Tōkai Nature Trail]]. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]] and Ōmiwa Shrine in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]]. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.

* {{ill|Anashi Izasu Hyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashi Izasu Hyozu Shrine|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}

Additionally, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] contains similar descriptions in its accounts of Emperors Sujin and Keikō. These descriptions further support the idea that Yamanobe-no-Michi had already been established during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century CE){{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

Archaeological findings suggest that it may have existed as early as the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], connecting the {{ill|Furu Ruins|ja|布留遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furu Ruins|qq=}} and {{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纏向遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku ruins|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向遺跡}}.

[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|A monument inscribed with a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]] poem by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]]. Located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|lt=Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社_(天理市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282589}} in Tenri City.]]

Stretching approximately 35 kilometers and measuring less than 2 meters wide, the road passes through key historical sites such as [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]], [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]], and the [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]. These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.

=== Course ===

The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the [[:en:Tōkai_Nature_Trail|Tōkai Nature Trail]]. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]] and Ōmiwa Shrine in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]]. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.

[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi near [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi]]

'''Yamanobe-no-Michi''' (山辺の道, {{small|Ancient Pronunciation}}: Yamanobe-no-Michi, {{small|Classical Writing}}: '''山辺道''', {{small|Old-Character Style}}: '''山邊道''') is one of {{ill|Japan's ancient roads|ja|日本の古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's ancient roads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a representative example of {{ill|Yamato's ancient roads|ja|大和の古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato's ancient roads|1=Yamato's ancient roads|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433382}}. It refers to a road that traversed the foothills of ancient {{ill|Yamato|ja|大和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato|1=Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13417196}} (a region known as Yamanobe, the origin of [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]]). It is recognized as the '''oldest road in Japanese history''' and as the '''oldest existing road in Japan'''{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

The road runs from the foothills of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]], in the southeast of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], to the foothills of [[:en:Kasugayama|Kasuga Mountain]] in the northeast. It follows the eastern edge of the basin, skirting the foothills along the Kasuga Fault Escarpment in a north-south direction.

Though some local government authorities use the variant spelling "Yama-no-be no Michi" (山の辺の道), it does not adhere to historical or [[:en:Toponymy|toponymy]] conventions. In contrast, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] uses the classical spelling "Yamanobe-michi" or "Yamabe-michi"{{efn|Example: The Imperial Household Agency manages [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] such as {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, officially referred to as "Yamabe-michi Makari-no-Oka Kami-no-Misasagi" (山辺道勾岡上陵)}}.

{{Quotation|The imperial mausoleum was constructed along the Yamabe-no-Michi.|Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin Chapter}}

This indicates that Yamanobe-no-Michi was established before Emperor Sujin's mausoleum was built.

Additionally, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] contains similar descriptions in its accounts of Emperors Sujin and Keikō. These descriptions further support the idea that Yamanobe-no-Michi had already been established during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century CE){{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

Archaeological findings suggest that it may have existed as early as the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], connecting the {{ill|Furu Ruins|ja|布留遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furu Ruins|qq=}} and {{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纏向遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku ruins|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向遺跡}}.

[[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|lt=Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社_(天理市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282589}} in Tenri City.]]

Stretching approximately 35 kilometers and measuring less than 2 meters wide, the road passes through key historical sites such as [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]], [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]], and the [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]. These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.

=== Course ===

The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the [[:en:Tōkai_Nature_Trail|Tōkai Nature Trail]]. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]] and Ōmiwa Shrine in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]]. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山辺の道]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11470977]]
Wikidata name: Yamanobe-no-michi
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Citation needed|date=June 8, 2012}}
{{Expand Japanese}}
[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi near [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi]]

'''Yamanobe-no-Michi''' (山辺の道, {{small|Ancient Pronunciation}}: Yamanobe-no-Michi, {{small|Classical Writing}}: '''山辺道''', {{small|Old-Character Style}}: '''山邊道''') is one of {{ill|Japan's ancient roads|ja|日本の古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's ancient roads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a representative example of {{ill|Yamato's ancient roads|ja|大和の古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato's ancient roads|1=Yamato's ancient roads|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433382}}. It refers to a road that traversed the foothills of ancient {{ill|Yamato|ja|大和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato|1=Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13417196}} (a region known as Yamanobe, the origin of [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]]). It is recognized as the '''oldest road in Japanese history''' and as the '''oldest existing road in Japan'''{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

The road runs from the foothills of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]], in the southeast of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], to the foothills of [[:en:Kasugayama|Kasuga Mountain]] in the northeast. It follows the eastern edge of the basin, skirting the foothills along the Kasuga Fault Escarpment in a north-south direction.

Though some local government authorities use the variant spelling "Yama-no-be no Michi" (山の辺の道), it does not adhere to historical or [[:en:Toponymy|toponymy]] conventions. In contrast, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] uses the classical spelling "Yamanobe-michi" or "Yamabe-michi"{{efn|Example: The Imperial Household Agency manages [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] such as {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, officially referred to as "Yamabe-michi Makari-no-Oka Kami-no-Misasagi" (山辺道勾岡上陵)}}.

== Overview ==

The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] includes the following reference in the section about Emperor Sujin:

{{Quotation|The imperial mausoleum was constructed along the Yamabe-no-Michi.|Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin Chapter}}

This indicates that Yamanobe-no-Michi was established before Emperor Sujin's mausoleum was built.

Additionally, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] contains similar descriptions in its accounts of Emperors Sujin and Keikō. These descriptions further support the idea that Yamanobe-no-Michi had already been established during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century CE){{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

Archaeological findings suggest that it may have existed as early as the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], connecting the {{ill|Furu Ruins|ja|布留遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furu Ruins|qq=}} and {{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纏向遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku ruins|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向遺跡}}.

[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|A monument inscribed with a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]] poem by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]]. Located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|lt=Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社_(天理市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282589}} in Tenri City.]]

Stretching approximately 35 kilometers and measuring less than 2 meters wide, the road passes through key historical sites such as [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]], [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]], and the [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]. These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.

=== Course ===

The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the [[:en:Tōkai_Nature_Trail|Tōkai Nature Trail]]. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]] and Ōmiwa Shrine in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]]. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.

== Notable Sites and Historic Landmarks Along the Route ==
* [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]
* {{ill|Ruins of Uchiyama Eikyū-ji|ja|内山永久寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruins of Uchiyama Eikyū-ji|1=Uchiyama Eikyū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393985}}
* [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Yamato Shrine]]
* {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qq=}}
* [[:en:Chōgaku-ji|Chōgaku-ji]]
* {{ill|Tomb of Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Emperor Sujin|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=崇神天皇|1=Sujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q333530}}
* [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]]
* [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]
* {{ill|Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|lt=Tenjinyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Shibuya Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|lt=Tomb of Emperor Keikō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukoyama Kofun|1=Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561706}}
* [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]
* {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|lt=Hibara Shrine (Ōmiwa Shrine Area)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}
* {{ill|Anashi Izasu Hyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashi Izasu Hyozu Shrine|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}
* {{ill|Yatogi Shrine|ja|夜都伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatogi Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=夜都岐神社|1=Yatsugi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431328}}
* [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]]
* [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]
* {{ill|Takenouchi Moated Settlement|ja|竹之内環濠集落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Moated Settlement|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kayō Moated Settlement|ja|萱生環濠集落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayō Moated Settlement|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shiki Mizugaki Palace|ja|磯城瑞籬宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mizugaki Palace|1=Shiki Mizugaki Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587954}}

[[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|lt=Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社_(天理市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282589}} in Tenri City.]]

Stretching approximately 35 kilometers and measuring less than 2 meters wide, the road passes through key historical sites such as [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]], [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]], and the [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]. These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.

=== Course ===

The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the [[:en:Tōkai_Nature_Trail|Tōkai Nature Trail]]. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]] and Ōmiwa Shrine in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]]. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山辺の道]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11470977]]
Wikidata name: Yamanobe-no-michi
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Citation needed|date=June 8, 2012}}
{{Expand Japanese}}
[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi near [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]'s [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi]]

'''Yamanobe-no-Michi''' (山辺の道, {{small|Ancient Pronunciation}}: Yamanobe-no-Michi, {{small|Classical Writing}}: '''山辺道''', {{small|Old-Character Style}}: '''山邊道''') is one of {{ill|Japan's ancient roads|ja|日本の古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's ancient roads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a representative example of {{ill|Yamato's ancient roads|ja|大和の古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato's ancient roads|1=Yamato's ancient roads|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433382}}. It refers to a road that traversed the foothills of ancient {{ill|Yamato|ja|大和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato|1=Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13417196}} (a region known as Yamanobe, the origin of [[:en:Yamabe District, Nara|Yamanobe District]]). It is recognized as the '''oldest road in Japanese history''' and as the '''oldest existing road in Japan'''{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

The road runs from the foothills of [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]], in the southeast of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]], to the foothills of [[:en:Kasugayama|Kasuga Mountain]] in the northeast. It follows the eastern edge of the basin, skirting the foothills along the Kasuga Fault Escarpment in a north-south direction.

Though some local government authorities use the variant spelling "Yama-no-be no Michi" (山の辺の道), it does not adhere to historical or [[:en:Toponymy|toponymy]] conventions. In contrast, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] uses the classical spelling "Yamanobe-michi" or "Yamabe-michi"{{efn|Example: The Imperial Household Agency manages [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] such as {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, officially referred to as "Yamabe-michi Makari-no-Oka Kami-no-Misasagi" (山辺道勾岡上陵)}}.

== Overview ==

The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] includes the following reference in the section about Emperor Sujin:

{{Quotation|The imperial mausoleum was constructed along the Yamabe-no-Michi.|Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin Chapter}}

This indicates that Yamanobe-no-Michi was established before Emperor Sujin's mausoleum was built.

Additionally, the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] contains similar descriptions in its accounts of Emperors Sujin and Keikō. These descriptions further support the idea that Yamanobe-no-Michi had already been established during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (4th century CE){{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

Archaeological findings suggest that it may have existed as early as the late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]], connecting the {{ill|Furu Ruins|ja|布留遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furu Ruins|qq=}} and {{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纏向遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku ruins|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向遺跡}}.

[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|A monument inscribed with a [[:en:Man'yōshū|Man'yōshū]] poem by [[:en:Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro]]. Located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]].]]
[[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|lt=Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社_(天理市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282589}} in Tenri City.]]

Stretching approximately 35 kilometers and measuring less than 2 meters wide, the road passes through key historical sites such as [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]], [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]], and the [[:en:Japanese imperial tombs|Japanese imperial tombs]]. These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.

=== Course ===

The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the [[:en:Tōkai_Nature_Trail|Tōkai Nature Trail]]. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri]] and Ōmiwa Shrine in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai]]. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.

== Notable Sites and Historic Landmarks Along the Route ==
* [[:en:Isonokami Shrine|Isonokami Shrine]]
* {{ill|Ruins of Uchiyama Eikyū-ji|ja|内山永久寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruins of Uchiyama Eikyū-ji|1=Uchiyama Eikyū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393985}}
* [[:en:Ōyamato Shrine|Yamato Shrine]]

* [[:en:Chōgaku-ji|Chōgaku-ji]]
* {{ill|Tomb of Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Emperor Sujin|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=崇神天皇|1=Sujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q333530}}
* [[:en:Kurozuka Kofun|Kurozuka Kofun]]
* [[:en:Kushiyama Kofun|Kushiyama Kofun]]
* {{ill|Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|lt=Tenjinyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Shibuya Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|lt=Tomb of Emperor Keikō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukoyama Kofun|1=Shibutani Mukaiyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561706}}
* [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]]
* {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|lt=Hibara Shrine (Ōmiwa Shrine Area)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}
* {{ill|Anashi Izasu Hyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashi Izasu Hyozu Shrine|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}
* {{ill|Yatogi Shrine|ja|夜都伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatogi Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=夜都岐神社|1=Yatsugi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431328}}
* [[:en:Mount Miwa|Mount Miwa]]
* [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]]
* {{ill|Takenouchi Moated Settlement|ja|竹之内環濠集落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Moated Settlement|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kayō Moated Settlement|ja|萱生環濠集落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayō Moated Settlement|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shiki Mizugaki Palace|ja|磯城瑞籬宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mizugaki Palace|1=Shiki Mizugaki Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587954}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山辺の道]]
